settings.title: Settings

landing.hero:
Your TV shows,
ranked your way
landing.subtitle: Create your personal ranking, rate every show, and discover what to watch next through users with similar taste.
landing.loginCta: Already have an account? Sign in
landing.feature1Desc: Search any TV show and add it to your personal list. Import from TV Time or MyAnimeList to get started quickly.
landing.feature2Desc: Rate each show from 1 to 10 with custom labels. Drag to reorder. Your list, your rules.
landing.feature3Desc: See how compatible you are with other users. Our algorithm compares ratings, rankings, and overlap.
landing.feature4Desc: Collaborative filtering finds shows loved by users similar to you — the more you rate, the better it gets.

faq.title: FAQ
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about TV Ranker

addShow.title: How do I add a show to my list?
addShow.body: Open **My List** from the sidebar. Click the **Add Show** button at the top. A dialog will appear — search for the TV show by name, then click the result to add it. The show will appear at the bottom of your list.

importExternal.title: How do I import shows from an external service?
importExternal.body:
From your list, click the **Import from external services** button in the toolbar. Choose the service you want to import from:

**TV Time** — Select a JSON file in Trakt.tv / TV Time format. You'll see a preview of the shows to import.

**MyAnimeList** — Export your anime list as XML from MyAnimeList (Profile → Export), then upload the XML file. Completed and currently watching anime will be imported, and each imported item is automatically tagged as **anime**.

In both cases, shows already in your list will be skipped automatically.

viewProfile.title: How do I view another user's profile?
viewProfile.body: Go to the **Explore** page from the sidebar and search for a username. Click on a user to visit their profile, where you can see their public list and your compatibility score.

copyList.title: How do I copy another user's list?
copyList.body: Visit another user's list. If your own list is empty, you'll see a **Copy list** button. Clicking it will copy all their shows into your list. This option is only available when your list has no shows — it's a quick way to start from a friend's collection.

rateShow.title: How do I rate a show?
rateShow.body: In your list, each show has a rating column. Click on it to select a rating from 1 to 10. You can customize the label for each rating level in your **Profile** settings (e.g., 1 = "Terrible", 10 = "Masterpiece").

reorder.title: How do I reorder shows in my list?
reorder.body: Each show has a drag handle on the left side (the grip icon). Click and hold it, then drag the show up or down to your desired position. The new order is saved automatically.

similarity.title: How do similarity, compatibility, and affinity work?
similarity.body:
The recommender system uses three distinct scores:

**Similarity (0–100%)** — Measures how similar two lists are. Calculated by combining three factors:

- **Overlap** — How many items you have in common, relative to the smaller list. If you share 8 out of 10 shows, the overlap is 80%.
- **Rating agreement** — For items both users have rated, how close are the ratings? A 1-point difference on a 1–10 scale is barely noticeable, while a 9-point gap means total disagreement.
- **Position agreement** — How similarly you rank items in your list.

Formula: `similarity = overlap × (50% rating closeness + 50% position closeness) × 100`

**Compatibility (0–100%)** — Measures how similar two profiles are overall. It's the average similarity across all lists that both profiles have actually compiled (non-empty). If one user hasn't built their movie list yet, only the show list similarity counts.

**Affinity (0–100%)** — Measures how well a specific show or movie matches a given profile. Used by the recommendations engine to score candidate titles based on how similar neighbours rate and rank them.

recommendations.title: How do show recommendations work?
recommendations.body:
The **Suggested for you** section on the Explore page uses collaborative filtering — the same technique used by Netflix and Spotify.

Here's how it works:

**1. Find compatible users** — We compare your list with all public lists using the similarity algorithm. The top 20 most compatible users become your "neighbors".

**2. Score candidate shows by affinity** — For each show your neighbors have that you don't, we calculate an affinity score based on how compatible the neighbor is with you and how highly they value that show (via rating or list position).

**3. Rank and suggest** — The 10 shows with the highest affinity scores are shown as recommendations.

The more shows you add and rate, the better your recommendations become. Shows that multiple compatible users agree on will score higher than shows recommended by a single user.
